enrichment.summary: Kyle records a rare sober morning vlog after three nights of only four hours of sleep. He discusses a possible opportunity to buy a window-washing business, his plans to sell his car to fund it, errands with his mom, and his decision to focus on his Bike Racer Levels website project rather than cycling.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: cautiously interested but reluctant to own a business long-term; sees it as a stepping stone he could pass off
wants to be a professional athlete and TV/movie personality, not a business owner
going all-in on Bike Racer Levels page over riding his bike, expects to burn out in ~3 weeks

stances.topic: Vindication of the Rights of Woman
stances.position: recommends it as a good book

entities: Vindication of the Rights of Woman

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder vlog for the 17th of January 2017. I'm actually sober right now. This is the first video I've done sober in, holy shit, I don't know how many years.

Uh, so hi, my name's Kyle, sober Kyle. What happened was, um, I was working until about 3.30 this morning, and then I was gonna do stuff with my mom this morning, so I figured I'd go ahead and go to sleep so I could have like a full day today, so I got to sleep at like 3.30, but then I got up at like 7.30, and I couldn't sleep anymore, I just woke up.

Uh, so this is like 3 days in a row I've gotten 4 hours of sleep a night. Yesterday I was totally, I was never, uh, fully, full energy, because I got up, I got to sleep at like 2, and then I got up at like 5 or something, and I wasn't fully charged, but then I got my second wind and I felt great, and I had to kind of force myself to get to sleep at 3.30, but, uh, so now it's 8.56, I was just talking to, hey, you that I work with, and I might, I might take over the business, window washing.
I'd have to get my hands on money though, and I can't get a, I can't get any kind of bank loan, because I don't make enough money, I can't prove that I make enough money, so.

I'd have to sell my car and then borrow money from my mom, um, maybe if I sell my car for 23, 11,000 in debt, then I'd have 12,000, and then, yeah, so we'll see, but, um, uh, today I'm gonna go and do errands with my mom, I'm gonna

Bring stuff over to my mom's house, and then Goodwill get empty out my car, and then I'm gonna, um, try to shampoo my car, and see if I can fix my rocket box, and get my, my car ready to go, ready to go, ready to sell, sitting in there, ready to drive off, and then sell my car for whatever I can sell it for, um, and then I'll see about if I can,
You know, buy the business. Um, I didn't really want to own a business though, um, so maybe I could pass it off to my roommate after a couple of years.

I want to be a, uh, professional athlete, that's my job, professional athlete slash, uh, uh, movie, movie, movie star, I want to be a, not, TV show, my, my, uh, 20 years TV series, um, and then, uh, yeah, and then do my web design stuff, but whatever.
This will be cool, eh, can you go?
So yesterday, I, um, I was working on Bike Racer Level Pages lists, um, and it's totally decimated right now, totally scattered, spread out, fixing different things, and basically a mess.

Um, yeah, I decided that I'm just going to go full, full bore on my website again. I thought that I was going to start riding my bike, but I think I'm just going to go full bore on the Bike Racer Levels page until I burn out on that.

I usually burn out after about three weeks, and I'll, I'll switch to something, if I do burn out on it before I'm done with it, I'll switch to something else surface level related, um, and try to get ads on my website and then attach words.
Um, yeah, so much to do. I didn't even finish. Enjoying the pictures. I have a pretty big room.
But, um, I was listening to V-Log, I mean, History Chicks some more yesterday, talking about biographies of famous women from the past, so.
Mary Wollstonecraft and them. Pretty good. Oh, that's a good book, by the way. Vendication of Rights of Women.
Um, and, um, yeah, so anyways, that's what I'm doing too. Alright, peace out.
